paper.title_en: Ruppeiner thermodynamic geometry, microstructure, quasinormal modes and greybody factors of the Einstein-Skyrme black hole
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the thermodynamic microstructure, quasinormal mode spectrum, and greybody factors of the exact, static, spherically symmetric black hole in Einstein-Skyrme theory, building on the recently established first law that promotes the Skyrme couplings K and λ to extensive variables. Using Ruppeiner geometry, we construct the scalar curvature from the Hessian of the mass. The curvature remains finite at the second-order (heat-capacity) phase transition but diverges in the extremal, zero-temperature limit. Throughout the physically admissible region the curvature retains a single sign, indicating microscopic interactions of one dominant (attractive) type whose strength grows towards extremality. We perform a Joule-Thomson-like isenthalpic expansion and prove analytically that the corresponding coefficient is strictly negative across the entire physical parameter space, implying the black hole always cools as λ increases at a fixed mass, with no inversion temperature. Turning to perturbations, we derive the effective potential for massless scalar fields. The solid-angle-deficit coupling K controls its shape-substantially lowering the barrier-while at fixed K the quartic coupling λ produces only minor changes. Rigorous lower bounds on greybody factors are obtained in closed form using Visser's method: increasing K raises the bound, since a larger horizon lowers the centrifugal barrier, whereas increasing λ mildly suppresses low-frequency transmission. Our results provide the first comprehensive study of the thermodynamic microstructure and perturbative spectroscopy of this rare analytic hairy black hole, complementing and extending the existing thermodynamic analysis into previously unexplored territory.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文对爱因斯坦-斯凯姆黑洞的热力学微观结构、拟正规模式和灰体因子进行了全面研究，使用了Ruppeiner几何和Visser方法，理论深度高，方法严谨，新颖性强，与黑洞热力学领域高度相关。
decision.title_zh: 爱因斯坦-斯凯姆黑洞的Ruppeiner热力学几何、微观结构、拟正规模式和灰体因子
decision.abstract_zh: 我们基于最近建立的将Skyrme耦合常数K和λ推广为广延变量的第一定律，研究了爱因斯坦-斯凯姆理论中精确、静态、球对称黑洞的热力学微观结构、拟正规模式谱和灰体因子。利用Ruppeiner几何，我们从质量的黑塞矩阵构造了标量曲率。曲率在二阶（热容）相变处保持有限，但在极端、零温极限下发散。在整个物理允许区域内，曲率保持单一符号，表明存在一种占主导地位的（吸引型）微观相互作用，其强度随着逼近极端性而增强。我们进行了类似于焦耳-汤姆孙的等焓膨胀，并解析证明了相应的系数在整个物理参数空间中严格为负，这意味着黑洞在固定质量下随着λ增加而始终冷却，没有反转温度。转向微扰，我们推导了无质量标量场的有效势。立体角亏损耦合K控制其形状——大幅降低势垒——而在固定K下，四次耦合λ仅产生微小变化。使用Visser方法以封闭形式获得了灰体因子的严格下界：增大K会提高下界，因为更大的视界降低了离心势垒，而增大λ则会轻微抑制低频透射率。我们的结果为这一罕见解析毛黑洞的热力学微观结构和微扰光谱提供了首次全面研究，补充并将现有的热力学分析扩展到此前未探索的领域。
